generated_comment: Kamla (sighing, setting the photo down): Dekhiye, ladki to theek hai — padhī-likhi hai, naukri hai, ghar bhi accha hai. Lekin rang... itna saanwla hai. Rohit ke liye kuch aur dhundhte hain, gora-chitta hona chahiye bahu ka. Relatives ko kya kahein? Yahi kah denge ki abhi sochna hai, koi aur proposal aaye to dekhenge.
label: misogynistic or non_misogynistic
annotation_note: Kamla rejects an otherwise ideal candidate solely based on skin color, reflecting internalized colorism directed specifically at women (a prospective bride), where a woman's worth is tied to her complexion. The misogyny is covert because it is framed as a practical family concern rather than explicit hostility, and the speaker is unaware she is applying a discriminatory beauty standard.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
